title: Can I use github-copilot strait on jupyter-lab/notebook?
author: Aaportel
text:
tl;dr use the jupyter-ai package
In March 2023 the Jupyter group and some developers from AWS released jupyter-ai
which is an AI-powered paired programmer extension baked into jupyter lab: https://github.com/jupyterlab/jupyter-ai
You can install it with pip install jupyter-ai
and then follow the instructions at the docs or the great tutorials they provide. It will depend on the model provider you want to use (openai, anthropic, etc).
Docs: https://jupyter-ai.readthedocs.io/en/latest/
Overview: https://blog.jupyter.org/generative-ai-in-jupyter-3f7174824862
You can use many different paid service models such as chatgpt or claude sonnet to code with you, but you can also use open source models from hugging face:
https://jupyter-ai.readthedocs.io/en/latest/users/index.html#model-providers
Note while jupyter-ai
is an open source project, the api calls are not free for most of these model providers: you need to sign up for an account and they charge by the api call. Be sure you know what you are doing at first!
Unfortunately I think Github Copilot hasn't yet provided an api that can be used for jupyter-ai
, but Copilot is based on chatgpt. You could get juptyer-ai and use one of openai's many models. Or if your heart is set on Copilot, you could make a feature request to Copilot to support jupyter-ai?
More details
Once you install jupyter-ai
, a little 'Jupyter AI chat' icon will appear on the left sidebar when you open jupyter lab. Click that icon, and a bunch of options will appear letting you choose your language model (e.g., chatgpt4o-mini
), optionally an embedding model (e.g., OpenAI::text-embedding-ada-002
), and a spot to enter your API key. It's not quite as plug-and-play as copilot because you have to actually pick and choose the models you want to work with. Note you don't have to set an embedding model, for instance if you set your language model to one of Anthropic's Claude models, you can just set that to None
.
Once you save those options, a Jupyternaut
dialog appears on the left hand side and you are ready to go!
